model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Despite missing starting point guard Caleb Foster and center Patrick Ngongba, Duke remains the nation's top-ranked team with elite offensive and defensive efficiency. TCU's strong defense (rank 30) could keep the game competitive, but Duke's superior talent depth, led by National Player of the Year candidate Cameron Boozer, and their experience in close games throughout the ACC season should prove decisive. The Blue Devils' ability to win without key players, as shown in their ACC tournament run, gives them the edge over a TCU team that lacks comparable star power and offensive firepower.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs TCU Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is the clear favorite despite injury concerns, with significant advantages in overall team quality and talent depth.
evidence: Duke ranks #1 in both KenPom and Torvik with a 31-2 record, while TCU ranks #43 KenPom/#48 Torvik with a 22-11 record.
Duke has elite offensive efficiency (rank 4) and defensive efficiency (rank 1), while TCU's offense is weaker (rank 81-83) though defense is solid (rank 30).
Duke is missing starting PG Caleb Foster (foot fracture) and C Patrick Ngongba (foot injury), but has shown resilience by winning ACC tournament without them.
TCU's Big 12 experience provides toughness, but they lack the star power and offensive firepower to consistently challenge Duke's elite defense.
Historical 1 vs 9 matchups favor the higher seed, and Duke's overall talent disparity remains substantial even with injuries.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://dukewire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/duke/mens-basketball/2026/03/16/jon-scheyer-updates-status-caleb-foster-patrick-ngongba/89183971007/ and extracted 1898 characters of text.

result.text: Jon Scheyer updates status of Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Jon Scheyer updates status of Caleb Foster, Patrick Ngongba Brian Stultz Duke Wire Duke has won 11 straight games entering the NCAA Tournament, including running through the ACC tourney for its second straight title. That run was capped by a 74-70 victory over Virginia on Saturday despite being without two starters: Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba. As the Blue Devils start the NCAA Tournament on Thursday against Siena as the top overall seed, Jon Scheyer gave an update on the status of the two players. "Caleb is going to be a little while still," Scheyer said on Sunday. "Again, he's progressing well.” Foster fractured his foot in the final regular-season game against North Carolina and has been out since. Still, his impact on the bench continues to help the Blue Devils. "Caleb, I've never seen a guy shoot so much in a scooter," Scheyer said. "Multiple times a game, I have to ask him to shut up for one second just to get in a few coaching points. While Foster is out for some time, the news seems better on Ngongba’s potential return. "As far as Pat goes, I think he's been doing his therapy, working with the medical team,” Scheyer said. “They're just benchmarks. He just has to continue to take it – it's a day-by-day thing to see how he can handle the next step and whether that soreness goes away. I can tell you he's improving. I feel very positive about it, but I can't say what that means as far as later in the week in terms of timing or readiness, because we have to take it step by step." result.title: TCU Earns No. 9 Seed in 2026 NCAA Tournament - Heartland College Sports
result.snippet: The TCU Horned Frogs have landed a No. 9 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament , as announced by the selection committee on Sunday night. TCU is coming off of a 78-73 loss to Kansas in the quarterfinals of the Big 12 Tournament on Thursday in Kansas City. TCU will take on No. 8 seed Ohio State on Thursday. If they win, they will play the winner of No. 1 Duke and No. 16 Siena on Saturday. TCU 2025-26 ...
